description: Tolerance case: gold accepts {anchor: agent, embedsOther: true} (event-triggered agent with wf-tools) or {anchor: wf, embedsOther: true} (alert workflow whose investigation step is an embedded agent). Reasoning dominates but the event trigger makes either anchoring defensible; a fixed pipeline with no open-ended step is wrong. Ported from the intent-resolution spec (§5.6 agent-incident-investigator).

conversation.text: Build an incident investigator that watches for critical prod alerts, checks logs and metrics, decides which service is likely responsible, gathers more evidence as needed, and recommends rollback, opens an incident, or escalates. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [Do not approve any plan, setup card, or build confirmation.
If the assistant asks a clarifying question, give a minimal plausible answer.
Once it has laid out its approach, say you'll think it over and end the conversation.]

processExpectations: The agent proposes handling the investigation with an agent primitive in one of two accepted shapes: an n8n Agent triggered by alerts (possibly invoking workflows as tools), or a workflow whose investigation step is an embedded agent; a fixed pipeline with no open-ended investigation step is wrong.
The proposal grounds the design in the reasoning-dominated investigate-decide-act loop (evidence gathering that differs per incident), not in vague or incorrect justifications.
